metadata.description: Stride task management skills for AI agents

plugins.name: stride
plugins.description: Task lifecycle skills for Stride kanban: claiming, completing, creating tasks and goals, enriching tasks, workflow orchestration, subagent dispatch, and automatic hook execution via Claude Code hooks

plugins.name: stride-security-review
plugins.description: AI-powered security review via /stride-security-review:security-review. (Renamed from `security-review` in v2.0.0 to eliminate the namespace collision with Claude Code's built-in /security-review command — see CHANGELOG for migration notes.) Two scan modes: diff (default, PR-gating) and --full (codebase posture check, batched with cross-batch dedup and skipped-files reporting). Detects 10 universal vulnerability classes plus 5 MAESTRO-derived agentic-AI classes (prompt injection, tool abuse, agent trust boundary, model output execution, vector-store poisoning). Three framework rule packs ship at launch (Django/Python, Phoenix/Elixir, Rails/Ruby) plus a multi-platform CI/CD pipeline pack (8 platforms). Every finding carries CWE-ID and OWASP Top 10 references. Opt-in flags: --maestro (7-layer threat classification), --rci [N] (recursive criticism passes), --baseline (suppress acknowledged findings), --patches (surgical-fix unified diffs). Semantic analysis, not pattern matching. Loosely based on anthropics/claude-code-security-review.

plugins.name: stride-ideation
plugins.description: Turn a fuzzy idea into shipped Stride tasks via two slash commands. /stride-ideation:ideate drives an interactive round-based question loop (up to 4 batched AskUserQuestion per round, mandatory round-3 framing checkpoint, seven hard-gated requirements sections, advisory requirements-reviewer pass) and writes a committed requirements markdown doc — the terminal state, never auto-funnels into the next step. /stride-ideation:stridify (new in v0.2.0; merged from the previous /decompose + /ship pair) takes the requirements doc and runs the full pipeline end-to-end: validates the seven sections, preflights auth from .stride_auth.md, dispatches the requirements-decomposer subagent (canonical batch shape embedded; two-reason multi-goal split rule of code-coupling plus ~10-task soft cap; cross-goal claim ordering documented in decomposition_notes), stamps source_spec + source_spec_sha256, writes and commits a sibling timestamped batch JSON for audit, then strips local-audit fields and POSTs to /api/tasks/batch with token hygiene (no curl -v, no token in logs or errors), rendering the created G/W identifiers. Replaces superpowers:brainstorming for Stride projects; the two skills coexist for non-Stride projects where brainstorming remains the right fit.
